title: Psephellus brevifimbriatus
taxonomy: Psephellus brevifimbriatus is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Asteraceae. It is native to the Iberian Peninsula, specifically in Spain and Portugal. The genus Psephellus is a group of daisy-like plants that are closely related to other genera such as Pulicaria and Argyranthemum. Within the Asteraceae family, Psephellus brevifimbriatus is a member of the subfamily Carduoideae. This subfamily includes a diverse group of plants with various growth habits and flower structures.

intro: Psephellus brevifimbriatus, also known as the narrow-fringed scabious, is a perennial plant in the family Asteraceae, characterized by its small, yellow or white flowers and a hairy stem. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, making it a useful remedy for treating various conditions such as wounds, infections, and oxidative stress. The bioactive constituents of Psephellus brevifimbriatus include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The plant is traditionally used to make various herbal preparations, including teas, infusions, and tinctures, as well as being used in topical applications such as creams and ointments.
